Hey what's going everyone. So the last two points here split testing done right and tracking event pixel fires are going to be covered in depth by Paul Phipps who runs all of our traffic in house for us. And I just wanted to give you kind of the real real behind everything. So you saw the compression you saw the way we can make higher converting images. You understand that you have to compress things. Sorry, I mean shrink margins and whatnot so that you can make sure that the call to action is visible on the page. And that's like the funnilology basics and they really do matter because most people don't do the basics which is why they're still not advanced. This stuff what I'm what Paul is going to outline for you is honestly one of the biggest keys to the kingdom. It's one of the reasons that we've been able to make funnels that convert really well and it's it's like it's totally doing the boring work. Everyone thinks that like we immediately you know hit home runs every time and it doesn't work like that at all. And so I can't even remember if he uses mouse flow or hot jar. I've used them both the one that he trains you on is the one you should use because he's smart about why he picks what he picks. But the old faithful funnel is a funnel that I spent so much time and so much money going over. And what I would do is I would watch 100 mouse click through sessions and you can watch them on like 8x speed so you can see what's happening. But it will show you the hot areas on the page where people are slowing down versus speeding up. And so testimonial by testimonial I would swap people out until my entire page was read meaning people had to like really slowly go through it because they were so captivated by each of the testimonials. And so the same thing happens is like and you it a page is a lot like a funnel in that you have to go from the instead of going horizontally through the funnel you're going vertically through a page. And so it's like if people are immediately bouncing we have to fix page load load times and compress the images and make doodle compelling headlines. Right. That's going to be the first thing like in terms of look and feel after we've done that actually making sure that the words are things that people are interested in and the offer is good and that the subhead is good and that there's enough scarcity and all that kind of stuff is going to be the next thing that you're going to split test. And so if you're doing split testing the right way most people just like doing a B split test on clip onals and hope for the best. But like most times they're split testing the wrong thing right so they waste tons and tons of time split testing shit that honestly doesn't matter like button button colors really don't matter in the big picture like the first thing you split test is going to be a headline. Right. And so that should be the first thing you split test. And so one of the other things that I like if I'm going to go like totally crazy is I want to do in the very beginning two very different looking pages so not the same look but like different fonts different color schemes everything. And then the winner of that is the one that I'm going to start drilling down into rather than trying to do minor optimizations. You also want to make sure actually think they even stopped allowing this but they used to have. I think you could do like multiple tests the reality is that you really shouldn't do more than one test at a time otherwise you're just throwing shit on the wall and seeing what sticks. And so this is the work that we do again and again and again and we go back through and watch the sessions and we see people's mouses and we go through when someone's applying we can see what they're filling out and then we're like oh OK that question is sticking people let's get rid of it put something else there. That image people scroll past and then the exit so that's not one let's swap it out with this one right and so we keep doing this and that is the work side that makes amazing like high converting funnels and that's the that's the boring stuff but it's the stuff that makes you the most money. And so you should consider that like funnel homework for yourself is that every week or every other day or however long it takes for you to get 100 clicks to your page which shouldn't take more than a few days. You go and you review them because usually they'll record the last hundred and then once you have those hundred you want to look at them and you can watch them super fast and you can watch people actually go through your funnel and you can see if things don't load and you can see if things look weird and you can see if people balance at certain points. And ultimately you just want to keep getting it to the point where it just becomes this slow scroll because they're so interested and then they click the button and they opt in or they they apply and sign up. And so that's the that's the ugly truth is that once you learn the compression things and the basics of of increasing or decreasing low times and making sure that you can make compelling images and make sure that you have the widgets right and so it looks cool and all of that stuff. Once that's done. This is the work. This is the stuff that no one wants to do and it's the stuff that will separate you from everyone else. So use it wisely. The tracking event pixel fires is how you can track the stuff. So Masphillus at hot slash hot jar will. They will give they will give you they will track how far people went. You can usually also tell it by time duration if someone's like three minutes or four minutes on that it means that they probably opted in. If you see something that's 16 minutes means they open it up and then just like left. But usually those are going to be the ones and so I think we learn just as much from the people who go through is the people who don't go through at all. And it's important to watch both of those things. That being said it's good to have separate pixel firing so you can also see which ads are creating not only the most opt-ins but the most schedules and the most applications because for example for us I've got one of the things early on for the book funnel that I started noticing is that I started making these long content videos which some of you guys may have seen. My actual cost per like messenger opt-in was higher by like 2x on those videos but the purchase rate and schedule rate from those videos was way higher than my typical short things or images. And so that is the type of stuff that you want to be able to track and see so that you can say oh actually this is a big winner. It's actually like we're getting less clicks but the quality of the clicks is so much higher because there's so much more sold. And so that's where if you don't have that tracking set up you can't know and then you can't make good decisions. And so this is the foundation of advanced funneletics, advanced funnelology, your PhD in funnels and this is how it works. This is the game.
